subject: Public Health
title: Origins of the Black Death
description: One-minute skill: Origins of the Black Death.

hook.text: In 1347, ships from Crimea docked in Sicily. The sailors were dying—or already dead.

buildup.text: Yersinia pestis traveled from Central Asia along trade routes, carried by fleas on black rats.

discovery.text: The bacterium likely originated in marmot colonies on the steppes of modern-day Kyrgyzstan.

twist.text: Climate shifts and Mongol conquests disrupted rodent habitats, pushing plague toward human populations.

climax.text: Within five years, the Black Death would kill one-third to one-half of Europe's population.

punchline.text: The deadliest pandemic started with fleas on rats.

quiz.question: Where did the Black Death bacterium likely originate?
